_about: multi-model-team config. Backends (CLIs) and agents are first-class and individually enable/disable-able; routes pick a backend per task (first match wins). Edit this file to add a CLI, flip a backend/agent on or off, or change an agent's role. After editing agents, run node src/lib/gen-agents.mjs to regenerate agents/*.md.

defaults._comment: preset biases tier (budget|balanced|premium). fallback is the final hop. quota_fallback is the ordered backend chain tried when the chosen backend is unavailable/exhausted.

team._comment: Roles + defaults for the /team pipeline. native, agy and codex are EQUAL, interchangeable tools — any can be assigned to any subtask and any can be the verifier; nothing is hardcoded. 'dispatch_backends' = the eligible set the decomposer may assign per subtask (native is always available as the judgment/hard-line option; drop a backend here to exclude it). 'verifier' = the backend that reviews each result (any backend name, or 'native' for Claude judgment). 'caps' = default max parallel subtasks per backend (by name). 'tier_models' maps a plan tier to a concrete model so the native model scales with complexity. 'relay_model' = the model the thin relay/verify agents run on. ALL overridable per-invocation (cap spec 'N:gemini,M:codex,K:claude', or in-session 'verify with X' / 'use Y for the front-end'). Precedence: built-in default < this section < per-invocation args.

reasoning._comment: Roles + defaults for the /reasoning Fusion pipeline. 'panel' is YOUR configurable list of reasoning models — editing it changes which models participate in /reasoning by default (e.g. add a 4th model: ['opus','sonnet','gemini','codex']; or go CLI-only: ['gemini','codex']). Each token runs the SAME question in parallel; see docs/REASONING.md for the full token vocabulary (opus/sonnet/haiku/gemini/flash/codex and their aliases). 'judge' compares the panel into structured analysis; 'synthesizer' writes the unified answer (both 'native:<tier>' or a bare tier/backend token). 'cap' bounds total panelists. 'tier_models' maps a tier to a concrete native model; 'relay_model' is the model the thin CLI-relay agents run on. The panel (and judge/synthesizer) are user-configurable here; overridable further per-invocation via a panel spec like '2:gemini,opus,codex', or in-session 'judge with sonnet'. Precedence: built-in default < this section < per-invocation.

backends._comment: Each key is a backend name a route can target. 'enabled' gates use (disabled -> skipped, falls through to the next hop / native). 'kind' selects the invoker in src/lib/backends.mjs ('gemini' (agy) and 'codex' are implemented; 'opencode' is a config stub with no invoker yet, so enabling it simply falls through until an invoker is added). 'writable_extra' = flags used INSTEAD of 'extra' in /team --writable mode (full-auto).

agy._writable_note: Flags used INSTEAD of `extra` in /team --writable mode (full-auto: agy may write files + run commands in its worktree cwd). agy's --dangerously-skip-permissions already lets it act; the writable lane only differs by running in the worktree cwd. Same flags here = no sandbox swap needed for agy, just cwd.
agy._cost_note: tunable estimate, USD per 1000 output chars — for the HUD cost figure only

codex._writable_note: Flags used INSTEAD of `extra` in /team --writable mode. Full-auto: `--dangerously-bypass-approvals-and-sandbox` lets codex write files, run commands, and use the network — confined to the worktree cwd the orchestrator passes via --cwd. Drop --skip-git-repo-check since a worktree IS a git repo.
codex._cost_note: tunable estimate, USD per 1000 output chars — for the HUD cost figure only
codex._note: Invoked as `codex exec <flags> -` with the PROMPT delivered on STDIN (the `-` sentinel), not as a positional arg — sidesteps the Windows .cmd newline-truncation bug (see backends.mjs invokeCodex / PROBES.md). Non-interactive, prints only the final message to stdout (no winpty). models empty = codex's own config.toml default; set per-tier model IDs here to map cheap/standard. `-s read-only` (in `extra`) keeps it from modifying files; `/team --writable` swaps to `writable_extra` (full-auto).

opencode._note: Future backend. Same as codex — config stub until an invoker exists.

agents._comment: Claude Code subagents the model can spawn. 'enabled' controls whether agents/<name>.md is generated (disabled -> the .md is removed so Claude Code won't surface it). 'backend'+'tier' set where the agent relays; 'dispatch' is 'route' (let the router decide) or 'forced' (pin backend+tier). 'role' becomes the agent's description (what the model matches on). Regenerate with node src/lib/gen-agents.mjs after editing.

agy.role: Backend-agnostic dispatcher for STANDARD, VERIFIABLE coding and Gemini's edges where the result is compact or easy to verify: new React/UI components, CSS, SVG/animation, scaffolding, CRUD, REST endpoints, scripts, CLI tools, glue code, SQL, regex, configs, Dockerfiles, unit tests, fixtures, data transforms — plus web-search/doc summarization and bulk text ingestion. Explicitly NOT for reverse-engineering, IL2CPP/protobuf-RE, disassembly, FFI/unsafe, binary, injection/hooking, concurrency, protocol/KCP design, proc-macros, or anything systems-hard (those are Opus-only and never offloaded).

codex.role: Delegate code review, test-writing, and verification to the OpenAI Codex CLI (codex exec): review a diff or file for correctness, bugs, edge cases, and regressions; write or extend a unit/integration test suite; or verify that an implementation meets its spec and the tests actually pass. Best for focused, self-contained review/test/verify units where the output is checkable. Use when you specifically want Codex on review/test/verify work rather than the Gemini backend or native Claude. Not for reverse-engineering, IL2CPP/protobuf-RE, injection, or other systems-hard work (those stay native Opus).

routes._comment: HARD LINE: RE / injection / binary / systems-hard. ALWAYS Opus, checked first so nothing below can offload these.
routes.name: re-injection-heavy

routes._comment: Deep systems / concurrency / architecture — judgment-critical, not commodity.
routes.name: systems-complex

routes._comment: Gemini-exclusive: A/V must go to agy even if it also carries a judgment word, so it is checked before judgment-coding.
routes.name: multimodal

routes._comment: Judgment coding — ordered ABOVE the commodity agy rules so refactor/bugfix/integration go to Sonnet even when they also mention a button/script/config. (review/unit-test moved to the codex lane just below.)
routes.name: judgment-coding

routes._comment: Codex's lane — code review, test-writing, and verification: code-specialized, checkable work that sits BETWEEN agy-Standard and native-Sonnet. Ordered below judgment-coding (a refactor/bugfix word still wins -> Sonnet) and below the OPUS hard-line/systems rules (those win -> Opus), but ABOVE the commodity agy rules, so a PURE review/test/verify task lands on codex.
routes.name: code-review-test

routes._comment: bulk-ingest BEFORE grounded-research: both match summarize/extract/ingest/scan; the cheap large-input path must win first or a 30k dump would route standard.
routes.name: bulk-ingest

routes._comment: Standard, verifiable coding — most everyday code work.
routes.name: standard-coding

routes._comment: Default when UNCLASSIFIED — prefer sonnet (safe middle), never agy on a guess.
routes.name: catch-all-safe

proactive._comment: Two opt-in nudges, both gated by `enabled` (off by default). (1) UserPromptSubmit: a submitted prompt that routes to a CLI backend gets a one-shot reminder nudging delegation. (2) PreToolUse Task/Agent spawn guard (guard_spawns): when you spawn an agent OUTSIDE /team whose task routes to agy/codex, nudge (or, with enforce_spawns, hard-deny) so it dispatches via the CLI. Deterministic firing; nudge = soft compliance. max_chars/min_chars bound the task size (0 = no bound); rules is a CSV allowlist of route names (empty = any agy/codex route). The plugin's own /team workers are exempt from the spawn guard.
